behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is model judgment quality plus decision handoff: whether an explicit image is actually recognized by the real vision model. A stub would hide misses. Rejected Workflow because behavior text names one model decision path ("image ... is flagged") rather than multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real vision moderation model on a fixed explicit-image canary set, then assert each item is marked flagged and lands in hold-for-review state, with no publish event emitted.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model robustness to obfuscation; deterministic fixtures would always pass and miss true classification failures. Rejected Workflow because behavior text targets a single text-classification decision, not a multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fixed hard-case canary suite of obfuscated hate-speech variants against the real text moderation model and assert all are flagged and held for review.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module orchestration/state handoff (two moderation results -> final publish path). It does not require real model-quality validation if both checks are forced to pass. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess behavior; rejected Agentic/Workflow because it is about decision wiring after checks, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire moderation orchestrator, persistence/queue, and publisher modules in-process; stub image/text check services to return pass; submit a post and assert exactly one publish record/event and no hold/drop state.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end SLA across multiple agentic steps in sequence (vision + text moderation) plus final decision. In-process stubs can hide real latency/queuing behavior from actual model calls, so lower levels miss real failures.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real submission path with real vision and text moderation calls enabled, measure end-to-end elapsed time from submit to terminal decision, and assert p95 <= 5s for representative peak-hour load samples.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets failure handling between moderation client and retry queue/decision logic. A controlled dependency failure (timeout/error) from a fake client is sufficient to catch wrong auto-approve behavior. Rejected System because real network outage is not required to reproduce this specific handoff failure; rejected Agentic/Workflow because no model-content judgment is under test.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestrator + retry queue modules together; inject vision-client unavailability errors; assert image posts enter retry queue with retry metadata and are never marked approved/published.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: Consistency risk is tied to real model behavior on borderline inputs (non-determinism/calibration drift) and cannot be validated with constant mocks. Rejected Workflow because behavior text centers on repeated evaluations of one image-classification stage, not a multi-stage agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline-image canary repeatedly within the defined short window through the real vision moderation path; assert decision consistency rate meets threshold and downstream state (hold/publish) is stable across repeats.
